constraints: I only work Tuesday through Friday.
Weekly status email by Monday 9:00 AM, max 150 words.
Manager prefers concise formatting — ideally up to four bullet points rather than long paragraphs.
Establish an after-hours boundary with a fixed cutoff time for checking work messages.
decisions: Define a clear after-hours cutoff time and communicate it to the team.
Create an emergency protocol for urgent issues (define what counts as urgent and how to reach you).
Pilot a phased offline trial for 2–3 nights to test boundaries.
open: Morning: pick 1 MIT and do 25–50 min focused work before email/notifications.
Schedule: two short admin/email blocks (mid-morning + late afternoon).
New task rule: Do now if ≤15 min / Schedule / Delegate / Decline.
Use a 2-min breathing reset or 5-min walk when stressed.
Wed prep (2:50–3:05): pick 1 priority + 1 question for 3:10 1:1 with Priya.
Friday before leaving: draft 3–4 bullets (progress, blockers, next steps) and save as Monday draft.
Monday 8:30: open draft, trim to ≤150 words and ≤4 bullets, send by 9:00.
End-of-day 5-min ritual: clear workspace and list tomorrow’s top 3.
Weekly review (Fri, 10–15 min): note recurring stressors and one small fix to try next week.
Mid-afternoon slump protocol: 5-minute walk (1–3 PM) + 3 deep breaths at end; pair with sleep and hydration for best effect.
Interruption management: draft a calm private conversation plan to discuss repeated interruptions with colleague.
Prepare a short script to address interruptions with colleague (≤30 seconds) as a reference.
Between calls: implement a brief reset ritual (60-second micro ritual: box breaths, hydration, light shoulder stretch, one-line intention, window gaze).
Make one reset routine the default between calls.
Two-minute desk declutter routine (2:00) each morning + voice/phone-friendly script.
